notes.note:
Had a word with the SP last week on Friday and today as well. She wants to churn because she is not getting enough leads. We have a proposition for her to offer her $75 per month for at least 3 months. 
We prevented the churn over here, and the SP agreed to pay $75 per month for three months.

candidate_facts.source_quote: She wants to churn because she is not getting enough leads.
candidate_facts.confidence_note: Explicit statement of churn intent with stated reason (lead generation dissatisfaction).

candidate_facts.source_quote: She wants to churn because she is not getting enough leads.
candidate_facts.confidence_note: Direct statement of the customer's primary pain point driving churn consideration.

candidate_facts.value: Offered reduced pricing of $75/month for 3-month commitment to retain customer.
candidate_facts.source_quote: We have a proposition for her to offer her $75 per month for at least 3 months. We prevented the churn over here, and the SP agreed to pay $75 per month for three months.
candidate_facts.confidence_note: Explicit intervention with pricing concession that successfully prevented churn.

candidate_facts.value: Customer may re-churn after 3-month discounted pricing period expires if lead generation performance does not improve.
candidate_facts.source_quote: She wants to churn because she is not getting enough leads. We prevented the churn over here, and the SP agreed to pay $75 per month for three months.
candidate_facts.confidence_note: Underlying dissatisfaction with lead volume remains unresolved; pricing discount is temporary measure only.

extraction_meta.extractor_summary: Beauty services business (Browfection Studio) at active churn risk due to insufficient lead generation; AM retained customer via short-term pricing concession but underlying product/performance issue persists.
